title: Applied AI Engineer
company_focus: AI-powered insurance brokerage and risk management platform
description:
About WithCoverage:

WithCoverage replaces the traditional insurance brokerage
with AI-supercharged risk management designed for the modern
economy.

We partner with hundreds of high-growth, category-defining
companies, including GoPuff, Eight Sleep, Bombas, Chomps,
and Blank Street Coffee. Our clients span iconic consumer
brands, hospitality leaders, GCs, advanced manufacturers,
and next-generation defense contractors. They operate in
complex risk environments and need a partner that can move
at their speed.

Instead of a fragmented, manual brokerage stack, we have
built a new category: elite risk advisors operating on top
of proprietary technology. Our in-house Agency Management
System gives our team and AI agents full visibility into
policies, exposures, claims, billing, and commissions. This
platform enables deep automation, better decisions, and a
fundamentally higher standard of service.

WithCoverage was founded by JD Ross (co-founder of Opendoor)
and Max Brenner (Bain, Compound). We have raised over $43M
from leading investors including Sequoia, 8VC, Khosla
Ventures and Crystal Venture Partners.

Our ambition is not to build a better brokerage. It is to
redefine how risk is managed across the economy.

Why Join Us:

- Grow Faster – We’re scaling quickly, giving you significant
  opportunities to learn, lead, and shape your career and
  the company's future.
- Work That Matters – We protect the world’s most innovative
  brands: consumer icons, hospitality leaders, next-gen
  defense contractors, and US manufacturers.
- Redefine an Industry – Insurance is one of the largest,
  slowest-moving markets. We rewrite the playbook with
  proprietary technology, automation, and AI.
- Financial Rewards – We hire the best and invest in you. That
  means competitive comp, meaningful equity, and excellent
  benefits. We believe strongly in internal promotion and
  lay out a plan for everyone's career growth.

About The Role:

We're looking for an Applied AI Engineer to work directly
alongside our risk advisors and turn their most time-
consuming workflows into reliable AI-powered solutions.
You'll be embedded with the team that serves our
clients—understanding how they work, where they lose time,
and what would make them dramatically more effective.

This isn't a platform engineering role. While our AI Agent
Engineers build the core agent infrastructure, you'll
operate on top of it—identifying specific workflow problems,
designing AI solutions using LLMs and our internal tools,
and shipping them into production. You'll own the full loop:
problem discovery, solution design, implementation, and
iteration based on real usage.

You need to be technical—comfortable writing production code
and working with LLM APIs—but your superpower is translating
messy, human workflows into clean, automated systems. You'll
spend as much time understanding the problem as you do
building the solution.

The surface area here is massive. Insurance runs on
documents, emails, and expert judgment—exactly the kind of
work LLMs are built to accelerate. You'll have the autonomy
to find the highest-leverage opportunities and ship
solutions fast.

What You’ll Do:

- Embed With the Business. Sit with our risk advisors. Learn
  how they manage policies, handle renewals, process claims,
  and communicate with clients and carriers. Understand
  where time goes, where errors happen, and where AI can
  make the biggest difference.
- Build AI-Powered Workflow Solutions. Design and ship
  automations that solve real problems—using LLMs, our
  internal agent platform, and whatever tools get the job
  done. Own the full lifecycle from prototype to production
  deployment to ongoing improvement.
- Ship Fast, Iterate Faster. You're not building a roadmap six
  months out. You're identifying problems this week and
  shipping solutions next week. Move quickly, measure
  impact, and iterate based on what's actually working for
  the team.
- Bridge Engineering and Operations. Be the connective tissue
  between our engineering team and our risk advisors.
  Translate operational pain points into technical
  requirements. Feed patterns you see in the field back to
  the agent platform team so they can build better
  infrastructure for everyone.

Who You Are:

- 3+ years building software in production environments, with
  hands-on experience applying LLMs to real-world problems.
- You've built AI-powered solutions that real users depend on.
  You understand prompt engineering, LLM integration
  patterns, and the challenges of making AI reliable in
  production.
- Strong software engineering fundamentals: you write clean
  code, design sensible systems, and ship consistently.
- Comfortable across the stack. Experience with our core
  technologies is a plus: Node.js, GraphQL, Postgres,
  React/Next.js.
- High EQ and communication skills. You can sit with a non-
  technical team, understand their workflow deeply, and
  translate that into something you can build.
- Bias toward action. You'd rather ship something imperfect
  and iterate than spend weeks designing the perfect system.
- You think from first principles. You can navigate ambiguity,
  make tradeoff decisions, and figure out what to build when
  there's no playbook.
- You want ownership. You're excited by autonomy and
  accountability, not layers of process.

For candidates based in the United States, the expected pay
range for this position at the start of employment is
$180,000 – $275,000/year. Actual compensation will be
determined based on factors such as market location, job-
related skills, experience, and qualifications. This role
may also be eligible for additional variable compensation,
including quota-based incentives where applicable.
WithCoverage offers a comprehensive Total Rewards package
for full-time employees, which includes equity grants and a
robust suite of benefits.

What We Offer:

- Competitive compensation that may include equity
- Flexible paid time off
- Comprehensive benefit plans for medical, dental, vision,
  life, and disability
- Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs): Health Care FSA and
  Dependent Care FSA
- Commuter Savings Account
- Human Interest: 401(k) provider
- Time Off: Sick Leave, Family and Medical Leave, Flexible
  Time Off
- Paid Holidays: Observance of all major national holidays
- A curated in-office employee experience, designed to foster
  community, team connections, and innovation, that also
  includes catered lunches in the office on Fridays for in-
  office workers
- Collaborative, transparent, and fun culture
responsibilities: Embed with risk advisors to understand their workflows around policies, renewals, claims, and client communications to identify AI automation opportunities
Design and ship AI-powered workflow automations using LLMs, the internal agent platform, and other tools, owning the full lifecycle from prototype to production
Move quickly to identify problems and ship solutions, measuring impact and iterating based on real-world usage feedback
Bridge engineering and operations by translating operational pain points into technical requirements and feeding patterns back to the agent platform team
Apply prompt engineering and LLM integration patterns to make AI solutions reliable in production environments
Navigate ambiguity and make tradeoff decisions to build solutions for messy human workflows in the insurance domain
use_cases: Automating insurance policy management and renewal workflows
Processing and analyzing insurance claims using AI
Automating document-heavy insurance operations (emails, expert judgment, policy documents)
Streamlining client and carrier communications for risk advisors
Building AI-powered decision support for risk management advisors
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves directly building, designing, and
shipping AI-powered workflow solutions using LLMs and
internal agent infrastructure. The engineer owns the full
lifecycle from problem discovery to production deployment of
LLM-based automations, working hands-on with LLM APIs,
prompt engineering, and AI integration patterns.
